criteria.name: SDP request path exists in source
SDP reply path exists in source
MCPL request path exists in source
MCPL reply path exists in source
risk recommendation explicit
criteria.value: Make MCPL the default inter-core lookup data plane for Tier 4.28+; keep SDP code path as fallback until MCPL hardware smoke passes

router_entries.sdp_routing_mechanism: Monitor processor handles destination CPU selection via SDP header dest_port field
router_entries.mcpl_routing_mechanism: Hardware router matches 32-bit key against routing table entries

mcpl_entry_breakdown.context_core: match REQUEST/CONTEXT keys -> route to context core
mcpl_entry_breakdown.route_core: match REQUEST/ROUTE keys -> route to route core
mcpl_entry_breakdown.memory_core: match REQUEST/MEMORY keys -> route to memory core
mcpl_entry_breakdown.learning_core: match REPLY/* keys (mask 0xFFFF0000) -> route to learning core

drop_causes: Monitor processor SDP queue overflow (core sends faster than monitor routes)
Destination core mailbox full (32-entry limit per core)
Monitor processor crash or reset during routing
sdp.drop_behavior: Learning core times out; no automatic retry; request counted as timeout
sdp.drop_detectability: High — timeout counter increments, stale reply check may fire
sdp.mitigation: Reduce schedule density, add host-side pacing, or switch to MCPL
sdp.scaling_risk: Monitor processor is single bottleneck; risk increases with core count

drop_causes: Router table miss (no matching entry for key)
Router congestion (too many multicast packets in flight)
Multicast key collision with another application
mcpl.drop_behavior: Learning core times out; same timeout counter as SDP
mcpl.drop_detectability: High — timeout counter increments; router miss is silent hardware drop
mcpl.mitigation: Verify router entries loaded, use unique app_id in key, monitor router utilization
mcpl.scaling_risk: Hardware router is parallel and chip-wide; scales better than monitor processor

sdp.path: Source core -> monitor processor SDP queue -> monitor parses header -> monitor writes to dest core mailbox -> dest core CPU reads mailbox
sdp.bottleneck: Monitor processor CPU and mailbox delivery loop
sdp.notes: Monitor processor is shared across all 18 cores; contention increases latency

mcpl.path: Source core -> hardware router key match -> router forwards to target core(s) -> target core DMA receives packet -> Spin1API callback fires
mcpl.notes: No monitor involvement; router handles all keys in parallel; latency is deterministic

risk_assessment.sdp_maturity: PROVEN — 4.27a hardware pass on board 10.11.194.65 with 144/144 lookup requests/replies, zero timeouts
risk_assessment.mcpl_maturity: COMPILE-FEASIBLE + WIRED — 4.27d/f pass locally; all four profiles build; callback wired; router init defined
risk_assessment.mcpl_hardware_uncertainty: Router table load behavior not yet validated on actual SpiNNaker chip
risk_assessment.mcpl_risk_level: LOW for intra-chip; MEDIUM for inter-chip (untested)
risk_assessment.sdp_risk_level: LOW for 4-core; HIGH for scaling beyond 4 cores (monitor bottleneck)
risk_assessment.recommendation: Make MCPL the default inter-core lookup data plane for Tier 4.28+; keep SDP code path as fallback until MCPL hardware smoke passes

claim_boundary: Source-code-based protocol analysis only. NOT hardware timing measurements. NOT router-table hardware validation. NOT multi-chip scaling evidence.
